hypothesis: Replacing the template-based natural language prompt generation with prompts authored by domain-naive human users will reveal a systematic degradation in CHROMA's color accuracy (increased ∆E) that correlates specifically with the linguistic distance between user-generated descriptions and training templates, and that this degradation can be substantially recovered by fine-tuning only the encoder projection layer on a small set of human-authored prompt–structure pairs without unfreezing the LLM encoder, demonstrating that the projection layer is the primary site of domain mismatch between real-world language variation and the fixed semantic embedding space.
